reason: Backend clean architecture boundaries and repository/application/API separation for workflow control-plane changes.
PostgreSQL schema baseline and migration rules for lifecycle run topology changes.
Structured error mapping for dispatch and repository changes.
Rust backend quality conventions and DTO naming rules.
Workflow graph, lifecycle run, graph instance, assignment, frame, and runtime anchor invariants.
Activity graph execution contract that must remain intact for explicit lifecycle runs.
Subject association and run/agent anchor lookup contract for graphless subject execution.
Rust contract to generated TypeScript workflow for DTO shape changes.
Frontend generated DTO consumption and mapper boundaries for optional graph/assignment fields.
Frontend store update conventions for lifecycle/project/session stores.
Repository audit of current graph dependencies, optional anchor fields, and default freeform entrypoints.
